vulnerabilities.description: Deserialization of untrusted JSON data without validation can lead to arbitrary code execution or data manipulation.
Deserialization of untrusted data using Hessian2Input.readObject() can lead to remote code execution via gadget chains.
SQL injection vulnerability: unsanitized user input concatenated directly into SQL query via ${name} placeholder.
XML External Entity (XXE) injection: DocumentBuilderFactory is not configured to disable external entities, allowing XXE attacks.
SQL injection via string concatenation of columnName and columnType in DDL statement.
SQL injection via string concatenation of columnName and columnType into ALTER TABLE statement.
Improper neutralization of user input in SpEL expression allows arbitrary code execution via expression injection.
Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) via hardcoded URL; potential for injection if URL becomes user-controlled.
Potential SQL injection via startDate and endDate parameters passed directly to MyBatis mapper without sanitization.
Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) via hardcoded URI; potential for injection if URI becomes user-controlled.
Deserialization of untrusted JSON data using flexjson without type restrictions can lead to arbitrary code execution.
SQL injection via dynamic orderBy and sortDirection parameters passed directly to MyBatis mapper without sanitization.
Sensitive information (page content) is printed to console, potentially exposing data in logs.
Open redirect vulnerability: user-controlled 'returnUrl' parameter is used directly in sendRedirect() without validation.
Open redirect vulnerability: user-controlled 'returnUrl' parameter is used directly in sendRedirect() without validation.
Out-of-bounds read: param_index is only checked to be < 128, but array size is 50, allowing access to indices 50-127.
Potential null pointer dereference: 'apter' pointer is not checked for NULL before calling get_apter().
Deserialization of untrusted data via torch.load with weights_only=False allows arbitrary code execution.
Use of eval() on user-controlled input allows arbitrary code execution. An attacker can inject malicious Python code via transform_map_expr.
Use of eval() on user-controlled input allows arbitrary code execution. An attacker can inject malicious Python code via transform_map_expr.
XML External Entity (XXE) injection: DocumentBuilderFactory is not configured to disable external entities, allowing XXE attacks.
XML external entity (XXE) injection via DOCTYPE with external DTD from http://java.sun.com/dtd/properties.dtd
Error message includes exception details (e.getMessage()) which may leak sensitive information about the system or file paths.
SQL injection vulnerability in @Delete query: parameter name is directly concatenated into SQL without sanitization.
No null pointer check for param_value; dereferencing a null pointer causes undefined behavior.
No bounds checking on param_index; can access out-of-bounds array elements in config_params[50].
Deserialization of untrusted data using pickle.loads() from base64-decoded cookie allows arbitrary code execution.
Potential SQL injection via dynamic ORDER BY clause. The orderBy and sortDirection parameters are likely concatenated into SQL without sanitization.
SQL injection vulnerability: user-controlled column name is directly interpolated into SQL query without sanitization.
XML External Entity (XXE) injection: JAXB unmarshalling without disabling external entities allows reading local files or SSRF.
Potential XSS vulnerability if bio_content is rendered as HTML without sanitization.
Deserialization of untrusted data via pickle.loads() can lead to arbitrary code execution.
SQL injection vulnerability: group_by_column parameter is directly concatenated into SQL query without sanitization.
Hardcoded database credentials (username and password) in source code, exposing sensitive information.
Deserialization of untrusted JSON data using Flexjson without type restrictions can lead to arbitrary code execution.
Improper restriction of XML external entity references (XXE). SAXBuilder by default may resolve external entities, leading to information disclosure or SSRF.
SQL injection via unsanitized sort_by_column and order parameters directly concatenated into query.
Potential code injection via Velocity template rendering if dashboardTemplate contains untrusted user input.
Out-of-bounds write: strcpy copies param_len+1 bytes but buffer may not have enough space due to integer overflow in offset calculation.
Integer overflow: current_offset + param_len + 1 may overflow if param_len is large, bypassing the bounds check.
Potential SQL injection if username is used directly in a database query without sanitization.
Potential cross-site scripting if username is reflected in output without encoding.
Stack buffer overflow possible if output_buffer is stack-allocated and result_len exceeds its actual size.
Buffer access with incorrect length: memcpy uses result_len without verifying output_buffer's actual capacity.
Potential XML External Entity (XXE) injection if XML output is processed by an XML parser without disabling external entities.
No validation of input strings (ip_addr, netmask, gateway) for format or length before use.
Path traversal vulnerability: configPath is user-controlled and used directly in FileInputStream without validation.
XXE vulnerability: SAXReader is used without disabling external entity processing, allowing XML external entity injection.
Duplicate class definition may cause unexpected behavior or information exposure.
Potential buffer overflow if result_len > buffer_size without proper bounds checking before memcpy.
Integer overflow possible if result_len or buffer_size are manipulated to bypass size checks.
Missing boundary check for layer_index; direct array access may cause out-of-bounds read.
Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) - URL is user-controlled without validation, allowing requests to internal services.
No input validation on URL parameter, could lead to unexpected or malicious requests.

Hardcoded database credentials (username and password) in source code, allowing unauthorized access if code is exposed.

Password is stored in plaintext without hashing, exposing sensitive credentials.
No input validation on user-supplied URL or parameters, leading to potential injection attacks.
Path traversal via user input in file path construction.
OS command injection via unsanitized user input in subprocess call.
SQL injection via unsanitized user input in database query.

Hardcoded database credentials in source code could lead to unauthorized access.
No validation of metric_name, label_keys, label_values, or label_count; null pointers or negative count may cause crashes.

NULL pointer dereference if firmware_data or version is NULL, but function returns -1 before use.
